title: Cacalia divaricata
intro: Cacalia divaricata, also known as Borneo button or Asian button flower, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia divaricata include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Cacalia divaricata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to make teas, tinctures, and other preparations.
taxonomy: Cacalia divaricata is a species within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is native to East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea. The plant is also known as the variegated false aster or the Chinese false aster. Cacalia divaricata is often classified as a perennial herb, characterized by its unique foliage and small white flowers. Its classification is further supported by its position within the tribe Cacalieae, within the subfamily Asteroideae.

constituents: Cacalia divaricata, a plant native to East Asia, contains a variety of active constituents. These constituents include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.
